title: Ryan Coogler Wins Best Original Screenplay Oscar for 'Sinners'
text: Ryan Coogler has won the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay at the 98th Academy Awards ceremony held March 15, 2026, at the Dolby Theatre. This marks his first Oscar win in this category and his fifth overall nomination. About the Win: "Sinners" defeated screenplays from "Blue Moon," "It Was Just An Accident," "Marty Supreme," and "Sentimental Value." With this victory, Coogler becomes only the second African American writer to win Best Original Screenplay, following Jordan Peele's win for "Get Out," and the sixth to ever receive a nomination in this category throughout Academy history. The Film and Script: Coogler wrote "Sinners" as his first original story after working on IP-based projects and historical adaptations. The film blends Delta Blues elements from his family heritage with genre filmmaking, featuring dual roles for star Michael B. Jordan. According to the article, the script was crafted "with the feeling of a great song, with rhythm in every scene change and resonance in the simple, evocative dialogue." Additional Nominations: Beyond screenplay, "Sinners" received 16 total Oscar nominations. Coogler is also nominated for Best Director and Best Picture for the film. Coogler's Acceptance: During his acceptance speech, Coogler had the cast and crew stand for recognition. He later spoke backstage about his late Uncle James, crediting him with providing "the gift of music that permeates through 'Sinners'."
